title: Maternal high-fat diet modifies myelin organization, microglial interactions, and results in social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits in adolescent mouse offspring.
ehr_evidence: None. The study uses a laboratory mouse model.
summary: The study investigates the effects of maternal high-fat diet (mHFD) on myelin organization, microglial interactions, and behavioral outcomes in adolescent mouse offspring.
key_findings: mHFD results in neuroinflammation and hypomyelination in offspring
mHFD-exposed male offspring showed increased myelin density with reduced cytosolic myelin channel area in the corpus callosum
lower transcripts of myelination-associated genes (e.g., Igf1) in the hippocampus of male offspring
mHFD-exposed offspring presented loss of social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits
